channel.title: Jingxiao Cai's Blog
channel.description: Technical blog by Jingxiao Cai about ML infrastructure, distributed systems, self-hosted AI agents, debugging, automation, and production engineering.

item.title: When Cleanup Reveals the Real Contract: Bridge Gaps, Watchdogs, and Agent Delivery
item.description: Cleanup work exposed the difference between removing stale workflow state and proving that bridge-back, progress watchdog, and final delivery contracts were explicit.

item.title: The Checkpoint Is the Interface: Durable Handoffs for Long-Running Agent Work
item.description: Long-running agent work should not depend on chat memory alone. Treat the checkpoint as the interface between worker execution, parent review, and visible completion.

item.title: When a Dirty-Tree Alert Is Correct: Classify the Artifact Before You Commit
item.description: An automated repository-health alert exposed a boundary problem: classify disposable review scaffolding, retained evidence, and intentional source changes before acting.

item.title: When a Credential Drift Checker Mistakes a Placeholder for a Secret
item.description: A credential drift check flagged an inert placeholder as if it were an active secret. The better fix was to model active-vs-inactive credential state.

item.title: When Multiple Agents Share the Same Proof Surface
item.description: Staged proof is only low-risk when shared harnesses, fixtures, queues, and scratch state have owners, namespaces, collision checks, and cleanup rules.

item.title: When Reviewers Still Ask for Live Proof
item.description: A reviewer asking for live proof is not a permission grant; agents need to clarify the live-only concern and route production risk to explicit operator approval.

item.title: Proof Expires: Why Agent PR Evidence Needs a Refresh Window
item.description: Agent PR proof can be true and stale at the same time; cite old evidence with freshness labels, target revisions, and refresh triggers.

item.title: Proof Without Touching Production: A Safer PR Boundary for Agents
item.description: Agent PRs need behavior evidence, but production should not be the default proof surface; use staged harnesses, synthetic state, and honest proof boundaries first.

item.title: When Secrets Become References, Monitors Need Adapters
item.description: When credentials move from env strings to structured secret references, standalone monitors need compatibility adapters before they report missing credentials.

item.title: Before Opening Another Agent PR, Reduce the Queue First
item.description: Agent PR hygiene starts before the next branch: check upstream, consolidate overlapping fixes, close superseded work with pointers, and keep one review surface.

item.title: Why AI Cron Jobs Need Exact-Exec Drivers Instead of Freeform Agent Prompts
item.description: Updated with a monitor-timeout follow-up: exact-exec drivers need durable artifacts, layered timeout budgets, silent-success contracts, and permission-aware follow-up checks.

item.title: When the Reply Exists but the Thread Stayed Silent: An Agent-Ops Visibility Lesson
item.description: A chat-agent visibility lesson: when final answers stay private, completion needs an explicit visible-send target, bridge-back contract, and duplicate suppression.

item.title: When a True Alert Is Still the Wrong Page: An Agent-Ops Threshold Lesson
item.description: A technically true row-count alert became an alert-tuning lesson: record weak proxy crossings, but page only when they combine with byte pressure, latency pressure, cleanup pressure, or user-visible impact.

item.title: Handling Gemini Capacity Exhaustion: Fallback Lanes for Reliable Agent Workflows
item.description: When Gemini route health drifts, the hard part is not picking a random next model. It is classifying capacity, auth, upstream, and adapter failures before changing fallback policy.

item.title: When a Coding-Agent Route Drifts: Closing the Loop Without Premature Fixes
item.description: A degraded coding-agent lane is not automatically a local repair task. Classify it as passive watch, upstream wait, or a narrow adapter fix before changing fallback policy.

item.title: Local Semantic Memory on a 4-Core ARM VPS: How I Got OpenClaw Memory Search Working Without External APIs
item.description: How I got OpenClaw local memory search working on a small ARM VPS, now with safer rollout, stricter active-memory canary gates, session-list fast paths, and source hygiene.

item.title: When the Report Exists but Delivery Failed: An Agent-Ops Triage Pattern
item.description: A daily scan job generated its report, but the final delivery side effect failed. The fix was to replay the saved artifact, not rerun the whole workflow.

item.title: One Heading Level Broke the Nightly Build: Fixing Markdown Drift in Generated REM Reports
item.description: A generated Markdown report failed over one heading-level jump; the durable fix was testing each rendered output surface as its own artifact contract.

item.title: Why Custom Skills Don't Load in OpenClaw - A Historical Bug and Follow-Up
item.description: A historical custom-skill loading bug, refreshed with a generated-Markdown regression lesson and a standalone artifact-contract follow-up.

item.title: Long-Running Agent Work Needs a Bridge Back, Not Just a Background Thread
item.description: Detaching long-running agent work is not enough. The workflow also needs an admission gate, a validated return path, and audit feedback when coverage misses.

item.title: Gateway Restart Behavior: What OpenClaw Users Need to Know About Config Changes
item.description: Some OpenClaw config changes apply live. Others trigger gateway restarts. Now updated with rollback, health-monitor, and task-registry restore-gap lessons.

item.title: When SQLite Looks Empty but Isn’t: Reproducing Corrupt Task Registries Without Touching Prod
item.description: A self-hosted agent-ops debugging story: raw SQLite can still see rows while the runtime registry restore fails, so reproduce on copies before touching production.

item.title: The 10-Second Session List: Why Prefiltering Before Row Build Matters in Agent Gateways
item.description: A self-hosted agent-gateway performance lesson: if a tiny session-list request builds hundreds of rich rows before filtering, limit is too late to save you.

item.title: Closing External Threads Cleanly: An Agent-Ops Pattern
item.description: A lightweight agent-operations pattern for closing external threads cleanly: make constraints explicit, record the decision, finish the action, and define reopen criteria.

item.title: Design-Tool Integrations Need Capability Gates: Lessons from a Missing LLM Config
item.description: Why design-tool integrations need capability gates before LLM generation: validate inputs, route readiness, model config, and artifact proof early.

item.title: Treating AI Agent Updates Like Production Deployments: The Runbook Keeps Paying Off
item.description: Why self-hosted AI-agent updates need production-deployment discipline: preflight, backup, staged rollout, human activation, adoption scans, and verification.

item.title: Building Fail-Closed Stage Environments for AI Agents on a Small VPS
item.description: An OpenClaw stage-environment pattern for a small VPS: fail-closed testing, zero-production-secret bootstrap, detect-only catalog refresh, and a mock-to-real-to-higher-risk ladder.

item.title: Fail-Closing Agent Launches: Why Auth and Readiness Gates Should Block Before Tooling Starts
item.description: Why AI-agent tool launches should prove auth intent, isolate ambient credentials, check route readiness, and block before side effects when the launch contract is unhealthy.

item.title: LLM Panel Orchestration in OpenClaw: Config-Backed Routing, Timeout Classes, and Honest Dissent Without Chaos
item.description: How I run a config-backed multi-model panel in OpenClaw: launch guards, watchdog-backed waits, bridge-back delivery contracts, and honest dissent safely.

item.title: Modernizing Agent Skills Without Growing a Skill Jungle
item.description: How I modernized agent skills with problem-first discovery, intake gates, thin wrappers, package hygiene, capability gates, and consolidation instead of skill sprawl.

item.title: VPS OAuth Survival Guide: Google APIs Without a Browser
item.description: Complete tutorial on OAuth 2.0 for headless servers, now with a fail-closed readiness gate that links OAuth-backed automation checks to the broader agent-launch gate pattern.

item.title: The Nightly Build: How My Agent Runs Security Audits While I Sleep
item.description: My AI agent runs autonomous cron jobs every night—security audits, health checks, and documentation—now updated with the exact-exec driver lesson that prevents false-negative wrapper alerts from hiding real command success.

item.title: Declarative Change Propagation: How I Built a Self-Documenting Cron System
item.description: How I built a declarative change propagation system for cron automation: manifest-driven updates, contract-derived documentation blocks, and validation that keeps desired state from quietly drifting — now with a stage-validation ladder from mock to real to a narrow higher-risk lane.

item.title: The Supply Chain Attack on AI Agents: What OpenClaw Users Need to Know
item.description: A deep dive into malicious skills in AI agent platforms, now updated with the LiteLLM incident, the first named downstream victim report, LangChain/LangGraph vulnerabilities, exposed Ollama servers, and an approved-but-blocked lesson from Moltbook's unstable post-incident API surface.

item.title: When Startup Checks Lie: Rolling Back an OpenClaw Runtime Regression
item.description: A clean OpenClaw upgrade passed startup checks but regressed under real use. This incident report covers the rollback, the verifier false alarm, and the upgrade guardrails I kept.

item.title: Bigger Embeddings ≠ Better Memory: Why I Chose text-embedding-3-small for OpenClaw Remote Memory
item.description: After proving local memory search worked, I stabilized a remote memory-only lane in OpenClaw. The follow-up reinforced the same lesson: source discipline, lexical anchors, and hybrid retrieval mattered more than another round of model churn.

item.title: The LiteLLM Supply Chain Attack: What OpenClaw Users Need to Know
item.description: LiteLLM 1.82.7–1.82.8 were malicious PyPI releases tied to a compromised Trivy CI/CD path. This operator-focused write-up covers the fast audit, rotation, inspection, and pinning checklist for OpenClaw users.

item.title: The Hidden Input Limit: When "202K Context" Doesn't Mean 202K
item.description: A debugging story about hidden platform caps, now updated with Anthropic's March 2026 flat 1M Claude pricing change and why cheaper long context still doesn't eliminate API-level ceilings.

item.title: The Recovery Problem: Why Your AI Agent Needs an Undo Button
item.description: I run autonomous cron jobs with no built-in undo capability. When Moltbook's community started talking about recovery primitives, I realized that unattended automation needs a stronger recovery story.

item.title: Blog Post Sanitization Checklist: What to Redact Before Publishing
item.description: I created a sanitization checklist after nearly publishing sensitive deployment details. Here's what to redact, what to keep, and validation scripts for technical bloggers.

item.title: Building a Personal AI Assistant with Gmail - Morning Memo Automation
item.description: How I built an AI assistant to automate my morning email routine.

item.title: The OneDrive and MultCloud Story
item.description: How I migrated my cloud storage using MultCloud.

item.title: Google API Setup Guide
item.description: A comprehensive guide to setting up Google APIs for your projects.

item.title: Troubleshooting AI Agent Skills
item.description: Common issues and solutions when working with AI agent skills.

item.title: Migrating from WordPress to GitHub Pages
item.description: I used to run a blog on WordPress back in 2016-2017 during my PhD years at University of Oklahoma...

item.title: Welcome to My Blog
item.description: After several years in industry, I'm starting to share my learnings, thoughts, and experiences...
